title: Genesis PATCH_REGISTRY entry
description: Schema for one entry in vllm/sndr_core/dispatcher/registry.py::PATCH_REGISTRY. Validates structural correctness and surfaces typos / missing fields before they hit a live boot. Used by the operator-facing validate-patch-registry CLI and the pre-commit hook.

title.description: Short human-readable name. Shown in dispatcher matrix + doctor + explain.

env_flag.description: Master env variable. Must start with 'GENESIS_ENABLE_' or be a shared family flag.

default_on.description: True = applies even when env unset. Default OFF for new patches; flip to True only after stable lifecycle + at least one PROD release.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle state. New patches start as 'experimental'. Code removal requires prior 'retired' for at least one release. 'legacy' marks pre-dispatcher patches (P1-P46 series) that have been live in PROD before the registry existed; they have minimal metadata by design.

category.description: Free-form category for navigation / Phase 2 wiring/ subdir grouping.

credit.description: What + why. Long-form free text. Should mention upstream PR number, original author handle, and the bug class fixed. Genesis-original work credits Sandermage.

deprecation_note.description: Required when lifecycle == 'deprecated'. Explains why and what supersedes it.

experimental_note.description: Required when lifecycle == 'experimental'. Explains the unknown / risk.

research_note.description: Required when lifecycle == 'research'. Explains why kept as reference.

community_credit.description: Required when lifecycle == 'community'. Plugin origin / author.

stable_since.description: Genesis version where this patch reached stable lifecycle (e.g. 'v7.62.18').

stable_kind.description: Required when lifecycle == 'stable'. Selects which ratchet rules apply: text-patch uses TextPatcher + anchor_manifest path; runtime-hook uses production_validated_pins (min 2). See Consolidated Roadmap §8.3.

production_validated_pins.description: Required when stable_kind == 'runtime-hook'. List of (genesis_pin, vllm_pin) tuples proving the runtime-hook fires correctly across ≥2 independently-validated combinations.

since_version.description: Genesis version where this patch was first introduced.

deprecated_since.description: Genesis version where this patch became deprecated.

removal_planned.description: Genesis version where this patch will be removed (only valid with lifecycle == 'deprecated' or 'retired').

deprecated.description: DEPRECATED FIELD — legacy boolean. Use `lifecycle: deprecated` instead. Old entries still recognized via lifecycle.get_state() fallback.

superseded_by.description: Patch ID(s) that replace this one when deprecated.

retired_reason.description: Free-text rationale shown when lifecycle == 'retired'. Use when the retirement context is non-obvious (e.g. 'fla recurrent kernel can't serve single-seq prefill — see PN108 docstring TOMBSTONE).

retired_waiver.description: When true, exempts a `lifecycle: retired` entry from the iron-rule-#11 'retired must have superseded_by' check. Used for patches retired without a direct upstream replacement (no-longer-needed runtime hooks).

vllm_version_range.description: Top-level pin-range gate for retired patches that should only decide on a specific pin window. Example: '>=0.20.1rc1.dev16,<0.20.2rc1.dev338'. Distinct from applies_to.vllm_version_range — this top-level form gates the entire entry's decision, used by retired entries whose code stays in registry for audit trail.

upstream_pr.description: vllm-project/vllm PR/issue reference this patch tracks. int = legacy PR-number form; string = full URL or issue-number form (G4_* style); null = Genesis-original or no upstream tracker.

upstream_issue.description: Companion vllm-project/vllm ISSUE number for the upstream PR (e.g. PN346 tracks PR #43650 which closes issue #43559). normalize_upstream_pr deliberately rejects issue refs inside `upstream_pr`; this field is the sanctioned home for tracking-by-issue.

upstream_pr_relationship.description: Iron-rule-#11 categorization of how this patch relates to its `upstream_pr`. backport=byte-equivalent merge-on-merge; related_not_superseding=overlapping bug class but different files (status-based retire does not apply); different_approach=same goal, different implementation; vendor_port=vendor's external PR ported into Genesis; issue_only=upstream is an issue without merged code.

anchor_breaker_watch.description: Drift-watch metadata: a known upstream PR / refactor that, if merged into a future pin, would break this patch's text anchor(s). Surfaced at pin-bump audit time so the operator re-verifies the anchor before deploying. Informational; not enforced by validator.

pr.description: vllm-project/vllm PR number (or URL) whose merge would break this patch's anchor.

also.description: Companion refactor / second anchor-breaking change to watch alongside `pr`.

mitigation.description: How this patch already defends against the drift (e.g. multi-anchor variants covering pre/post-PR shapes).

composes_with.description: Patch IDs that participate in a designed composition graph with this one (companion patches that produce a complete capability when applied together). Informational; not enforced by validator. Used by `genesis explain` to surface co-applied surface.

notes.description: Free-form notes for operator-facing context. Reserve for tactical observations (e.g., 'bench tested 2026-05-XX on dev338'); deep credit/rationale belongs in `credit`.

retire_after_pin.description: Version pin string after which this patch should be retired. Operator-driven retirement (e.g., when this pin lands and upstream supersedes). Distinct from vllm_version_range (which is a hard gate); this is a documentation hint for the next pin-bump audit.

applies_to.description: Predicate that gates this patch by hardware / model / version. Either flat-dict legacy form (auto-normalized to all_of) or rich AND/OR/NOT/NONE_OF compound form. See compat/predicates.py.

requires_patches.description: Patch IDs that must also APPLY for this patch to make sense. Validated by A3/D2 validator at boot.

conflicts_with.description: Patch IDs that MUST NOT be applied at the same time. Validated by A3/D2 validator at boot.

tier.description: Stage 5 (2026-05-07): commercial-license tier. 'community' = MIT/Apache, lives in vllm/sndr_core/; 'engine' = commercial license, lives in vllm/sndr_engine/. The dispatcher.decision tier-gate skips tier='engine' entries when sndr_engine package is not installed.

family.description: Stage 5 (2026-05-07): engine subsystem the patch belongs to. Used by Stage 6 reorg (subsystem-first taxonomy of vllm/sndr_core/integrations/) and by `sndr family <X> status` CLI.

enables_upstream_feature.description: Iron-rule-#11 case (c) — 'different approach'. When True, the linked upstream PR adds a new experimental feature OFF by default and this Genesis patch turns it ON (with extra Genesis-side wiring). Not a retire candidate when upstream merges; excluded from the NEWLY-MERGED audit queue.

implementation_status.description: P1-2 audit closure 2026-05-08 (noonghunna): granular implementation state. 'full'/'live' = wired and active; 'placeholder'/'marker_only'/'partial' = registry exists but apply path TBD; 'retired'/'upstream_merged' = no longer active; 'research' = opt-in research path.

source.description: P1-2 audit closure 2026-05-08: provenance of the patch idea. Used by CREDITS.md / release-notes attribution. Inferred from upstream_pr / credit text when not set explicitly.

apply_module.description: Explicit dotted-path override for the apply module (e.g. 'vllm.sndr_core.patches.middleware.pn16_v6_streaming_truncator'). Set when the registry sub-id (e.g. 'PN16_V6') doesn't auto-resolve via the canonical pn{digit}_*.py regex. Most entries leave this unset and rely on auto-derivation in dispatcher/spec.py.

related_upstream_prs.description: Optional list of related vllm-project/vllm PR numbers when a Genesis backport touches multiple upstream PRs.
